case_title: CAPITAL ONE BANK VS. ZHI JIAN WU et al

HEARING ON APPLICANT/DEFENDANT ZHI WU'S WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS. NO APPEARANCE BY APPLICANT. THE COURT BEING UNABLE TO EXAMINE APPLICANT RULED ON THE APPLICATION SUBMITTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE APPLICANT'S INCOME IS ENOUGH TO PAY THE COMMON NECESSARIES OF LIFE AND COURT FEES AND COSTS.FEE WAIVER DENIED. ZHI WU HAS TEN DAYS TO PAY THE FILING FEES OR THE FILINGS WILL BE STRICKEN. ORDER DENYING WAIVER OF FEE SENT TO ALL PARTIES. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: E. BURA; REPORTER: SUSAN LEE, #4280 (D206)
NOTICE OF CHANGE OF ADDRESS FILED BY ATTORNEY WALSH, MARK D
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR (TRANSACTION ID # 100013968) FILED BY PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ZHI JIAN WU'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS GRANTED. IT IS FURTHER ORDERED THAT DEFENDANT SHALL PAY PLAINTIFF $50.00 EACH MONTH TOWARDS THE JUDGMENT. PAYMENTS SHALL BE MADE ON THE FIRST DAY OF EACH MONTH, AND MADE OUT TO AND SENT TO PLAINTIFF'S ATTORNEY-OF-RECORD. IF THE LEVYING OFFICER IS HOLDING ANY MONIES, THE FIRST $50.00 OF THOSE MONIES SHALL BE RELEASED TO THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR, AND THE REMAINDER OF THOSE MONIES SHALL BE RETURNED TO THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR. COURT TO PREPARE AND MAIL OUT AN ORDER TO PARTIES AND LEVYING OFFICER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
Motion & Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
Off Calendar Mar-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. SYDNEY E. FAIRBAIRN, ESQ., Tel No. 415-883-2199 specially appearing for the Plaintiff;
Defendant ZHI JIAN WU, Tel No. 415-244-9708 present in pro per;
QI HONG HUANG, Defendant's wife, present.

Matter argued and submitted by parties. The Court modifies its tentative ruling, and orders as follows: Defendant Zhi Jian Wu's Claim of Exemption is granted. It is further ordered that Defendant shall pay Plaintiff $50.00 each month towards the judgment. Payments shall be made on the first day of each month, and made out to and sent to Plaintiff's attorney-of-record. If the levying officer is holding any monies, the first $50.00 of those monies shall be released to the judgment creditor, and the remainder of those monies shall be returned to the judgment debtor. COURT TO PREPARE AND MAIL OUT AN ORDER TO PARTIES AND LEVYING OFFICER. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-02-2008 Dismissal Or Judgment On File As To All Defendants

HEARING ORDERED FOR APPLICATION OF WAIVER OF COURT FEES
Off Calendar May-14-2008 Denied
